entries.summary: Read, write, and search files inside an allow-listed directory.
entries.description: Provides safe filesystem access scoped to a workspace path you explicitly choose. Useful for letting agents read project files and write generated code without exposing the rest of your home directory.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

default_env.label: Workspace path
default_env.description: Directory the server is allowed to access.

entries.summary: Inspect git repositories — log, diff, blame, branches.
entries.description: Read-only git operations against any repository on disk. Pairs naturally with the Filesystem server when reviewing or summarising code.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

default_env.label: Repository path
default_env.description: Path to a git repository on disk.

entries.summary: Read and write GitHub issues, pull requests, and repository metadata.
entries.description: Talks to the GitHub REST API on your behalf. Useful for triaging issues, opening PRs, and reading review comments without leaving the agent.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

default_env.label: GitHub personal access token
default_env.description: Token used to authenticate against the GitHub API.

entries.summary: Browse and update GitLab projects, issues, and merge requests.
entries.description: Talks to a GitLab instance via its REST API. Suitable for self-hosted GitLab as well as gitlab.com via the API URL setting.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

default_env.label: GitLab personal access token
default_env.description: Token used to authenticate against the GitLab API.

default_env.label: GitLab API URL
default_env.description: Base URL of the GitLab API; change for self-hosted instances.

entries.summary: Search the web with Brave's privacy-focused search API.
entries.description: Returns ranked web results suitable for fact-checking, research, and grounding answers in current information.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

default_env.label: Brave Search API key
default_env.description: API key issued by the Brave Search developer portal.

entries.summary: Neural web search and structured content retrieval via Exa.
entries.description: Useful when you want semantic web search rather than pure keyword matching, or when you need page-level content extraction.
entries.author: Exa

default_env.label: Exa API key
default_env.description: API key from the Exa dashboard.

entries.summary: Web search and answer engine optimised for LLM grounding.
entries.description: Returns concise, citation-friendly search results. Convenient when you want a single search call to deliver both a synthesised answer and source URLs.
entries.author: Tavily

default_env.label: Tavily API key
default_env.description: API key from the Tavily dashboard.

entries.summary: Drive a headless Chromium browser via Puppeteer.
entries.description: Useful for screenshotting pages, evaluating JavaScript inside a real browser, and scraping content that requires DOM rendering.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

entries.summary: Cross-browser automation via Microsoft's Playwright runtime.
entries.description: Provides Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit automation for end-to-end browsing tasks, with structured a11y snapshots that pair well with LLMs.
entries.author: Microsoft / Playwright

entries.summary: Run read/write SQL queries against a local SQLite database.
entries.description: Great for inspecting application databases, prototyping schemas, or letting an agent answer questions over structured data on disk.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

default_env.label: Database path
default_env.description: Path to the SQLite database file the server should open.

entries.summary: Query a PostgreSQL database over the standard connection URL.
entries.description: Lets agents run SQL against an existing Postgres database. The server holds the connection string in memory; treat the URL as a secret.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

default_env.label: Postgres connection URL
default_env.description: libpq-style URL, e.g. postgres://user:password@host:5432/db.

entries.summary: Read and write keys against a Redis instance.
entries.description: Useful for cache inspection, queue debugging, or letting an agent populate test fixtures into Redis.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

default_env.label: Redis connection URL
default_env.description: redis:// URL pointing at the target instance.

entries.summary: Resolve the current time and convert between timezones.
entries.description: Tiny helper that gives the model accurate "now" plus timezone arithmetic. Avoids hallucinated timestamps in scheduling and logging tasks.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

entries.summary: Retrieve a URL and return cleaned-up text or markdown.
entries.description: A safe HTTP fetch that strips boilerplate from HTML pages so the model can read articles or API documentation without rendering JS.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

entries.summary: Reference server exercising every MCP capability — tools, resources, prompts.
entries.description: Useful as a smoke test when integrating Kairox with the protocol, or as a learning aid to inspect what an MCP server can expose.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

entries.summary: Knowledge-graph memory the model can read and write.
entries.description: Persists structured "facts" the model emits across sessions. Pairs nicely with Kairox's own memory store when you want a portable, MCP-side store.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

entries.summary: Read channels and post messages on Slack.
entries.description: Suitable for Slack-driven workflows: triaging incoming questions, posting status updates, or summarising long threads back into a channel.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

default_env.label: Slack bot token
default_env.description: xoxb- token from the Slack app's OAuth & Permissions page.

default_env.label: Slack team ID
default_env.description: Workspace ID (T...) the bot operates within.

entries.summary: Read and send Gmail messages with auto-OAuth setup.
entries.description: Community Gmail server that walks you through Google OAuth on first use. Useful for inbox triage and drafting replies from inside the agent.
entries.author: @gongrzhe (community)

entries.summary: Read and edit Notion pages, databases, and blocks.
entries.description: Useful for capturing meeting notes, syncing tasks into a Notion database, or letting agents browse internal docs hosted in Notion.
entries.author: Notion

default_env.label: Notion integration token
default_env.description: Internal integration token from notion.so/my-integrations.

entries.summary: Manage Linear issues, cycles, and projects.
entries.description: Talks to the Linear GraphQL API. Useful for triaging issues, transitioning workflow states, and producing release notes from completed cycles.
entries.author: @tacticlaunch (community)

default_env.label: Linear API key
default_env.description: Personal API key from Linear's settings → API.

entries.summary: Search and edit a local Obsidian vault via the Local REST API plugin.
entries.description: Lets agents read backlinks, query daily notes, and append content to your vault. Requires the Local REST API community plugin to be enabled inside Obsidian.
entries.author: MCP community

default_env.label: Obsidian Local REST API key
default_env.description: API key shown in the Local REST API plugin settings.

default_env.label: Obsidian Local REST API host
default_env.description: Base URL exposed by the Local REST API plugin.

entries.summary: Query AWS Bedrock Knowledge Base retrieval APIs.
entries.description: Performs retrieval-augmented lookups against an AWS Knowledge Base. Useful when your organisation already curates documents inside Bedrock.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

default_env.label: AWS access key ID
default_env.description: IAM access key with permission to call Bedrock retrieval APIs.

default_env.label: AWS secret access key
default_env.description: Secret part of the IAM credentials.

default_env.label: AWS region
default_env.description: Region the Knowledge Base lives in.

entries.summary: Geocode addresses and look up places via Google Maps APIs.
entries.description: Gives the model directions, place details, and geocoding without needing custom HTTP code. Suitable for location-aware assistants.
entries.author: Anthropic / MCP

default_env.label: Google Maps API key
default_env.description: API key with the Places, Geocoding, and Directions APIs enabled.

entries.summary: Inspect Sentry issues and events from inside the agent.
entries.description: Looks up issues, events, and stack traces against a Sentry organisation. Useful for triaging crash reports while pairing with the agent.
entries.author: Sentry / MCP

default_env.label: Sentry auth token
default_env.description: User auth token from sentry.io with read access to your org.
